detail: Determinate sentencing for offenses on or after December 31, 1999, replacing parole with extended supervision.
Refined truth-in-sentencing mechanics without adding any medical or geriatric release provision.
Created petitions for sentence modification on extraordinary health condition or geriatric status, overseen by the Earned Release Review Commission.
Repealed most early release mechanisms from Act 28, dissolved the review commission, and moved petitions to department administration.
Implemented extraordinary health condition and geriatric petitions, formalizing eligibility, documentation, and review.
Petition and order forms defined the age and time-served thresholds and set procedural uniformity.
source: Wisconsin Court System forms CR-254, CR-255, CR-256
source_detail: thresholds of 65 and 5 years, or 60 and 10 years

detail: Updated internal processes, documentation standards, committee roles, exclusions, and referral procedures.
Amended notification requirements and modernized cross-references without altering eligibility criteria.
